channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok starts testing Footnotes, a new feature that looks a lot like X’s Community Notes
item.description: TikTok is launching its own version of community notes on the platform, called “Footnotes.” The crowd-sourced approach to moderation, where users add additional context to posts, has become increasingly popular on social media networks after X (then called Twitter) first launched it in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg once floated spinning off Instagram over antitrust fears, email reveals in trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok Users Bypass Tariffs with Chinese Wholesale Videos
item.description: TikTok users are flooding their For You Pages with videos from Chinese manufacturers urging Americans to bypass tariffs by purchasing goods directly from China.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg underscores TikTok competition as Meta fights monopoly allegations
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized Wednesday that his social media company faces stiff competition from TikTok, as Meta seeks to fend off accusations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that it has a monopoly over social networking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: A colossal squid is caught on camera for the first time in the deep sea
item.description: A colossal squid has been captured on camera for the first time in the deep sea by an international team of researchers steering a remotely operated submersible.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This cheap projector has a gimbal stand and smart auto adjustments so it's easy to stream Netflix and Prime Video anywhere
item.description: 1080p at up to 120 inches from this super-small and attractively priced portable projector with streaming built-in
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Unlock Your Android Recall: Introducing Snapseek
item.description: Discover how Snapseek, a simple Android app, can enhance your memory and provide a sleek new searching superpower.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Headlamp: A multicluster Kubernetes user interface
item.description: As important as Kubernetes is to the modern cloud-native stack, it’s still not easy to use on your own hardware. That’s surprising, considering how long it’s been around.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Notion Mail lets you build the inbox you actually want
item.description: Most email apps have strong opinions about what your inbox should look like. Notion Mail is the opposite. The new offshoot from productivity app Notion is all about flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: A strong solar storm heads to Earth. Here's what to know about northern lights
item.description: A strong solar storm is headed to Earth and this could cause colorful aurora displays in parts of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg spars with FTC lawyer over meaning of emails cited in antitrust trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg sparred Tuesday with a Federal Trade Commission attorney over the meaning of emails the Facebook founder wrote about why he wanted to acquire Instagram, as a historic antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market went into its second day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Fewer Americans want government, tech companies restricting false content online: Poll
item.description: Support for the U.S. government or technology companies' attempts to restrict false information online dropped slightly among Americans over the past two years, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump's fluctuating tariffs stir confusion in tech industry
item.description: The Trump administration’s back-and-forth moves on tariffs for technology products are stirring confusion in a sector heavily reliant on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta antitrust trial tests MAGA-tech relationship
item.description: A major antitrust trial against Meta that kicked off Monday — with CEO Mark Zuckerberg taking the stand — is a test of how much tech giants can get out of the MAGA embrace that swept across the industry in the wake of President Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Smarter dairy farms where robots milk the cows
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how robots can milk, feed and clean cows on dairy farms, boosting efficiency and comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: CrowdStrike’s field tech strategist explains how AWS Control Tower plus CrowdStrike Falcon lets new workloads inherit access controls in zero trust environments The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: CrowdStrike’s Dennen Monks on ensuring none of your assets escape zero trust coverage first
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Groundcover grows funding for eBPF-based observability tech
item.description: When Groundcover entered the observability market in 2021, it was already a mature market with established vendors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation
